question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with a `start` and `end` timestamp spanning the full 24-hour window, causing the batch loop to execute thousands of DB round-trips (each fetching `batchSize=2000` rows), thereby delaying block processing by 500% or more of the average block time and temporarily freezing transaction visibility?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged user craft a timestamp range where `start` is just 1 nanosecond less than `end` but the range contains exactly `batchSize=2000` transactions per iteration, forcing the loop to never terminate early (since `len(transactionsBatch) == batchSize` always), causing indefinite DB polling that delays block finalization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the batch loop in `FindBetween` by providing a timestamp range that always returns exactly `batchSize` rows, causing `start` to advance by only 1 nanosecond per iteration (`start = transactionsBatch[len(transactionsBatch)-1].ConsensusTimestamp + 1`), resulting in O(N/2000) DB queries that cumulatively delay block processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged user cause a block delay exceeding 500% of average block time by submitting concurrent `FindBetween` requests with overlapping large timestamp ranges, saturating the DB connection pool via `tr.dbClient.GetDbWithContext(ctx)` and starving legitimate block-processing queries?
